sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Let me look at the left panel structure:
- id=5: white, 50px, top_left=[3,3] - this is the white rectangle in the left panel
- id=9: purple, 50px, top_left=[8,3] - this is the purple rectangle in the left panel
The left panel shows white on top, purple on bottom - a horizontal split.
The block has:
- id=12: purple, 84px, top_left=[33,18] - inside frame, left part
- id=13: purple, 65px, top_left=[34,27] - right part, upper
- id=14: white, 35px, top_left=[35,36] - right part, lower
The block is a diagonal split. I need to make it horizontal.
I've been trying SPACE with different colors but it only toggles between two diagonal states. Let me try a completely different approach.
What if I need to:
1. Move the frame to the UP position [21,24]
2. Merge the block (SPACE with white id=12)
3. Then do something different with the merged block
Or what if I need to click directly on the block cells with MOUSE to change individual cells?
Let me try clicking on the white part of the block (id=14, around row 35-39, col 36-43) to see if it changes color.
Actually, let me first try moving the frame UP and then pressing SPACE to see what happens with the merged state.
Wait, I already tried that. Let me try something completely different - clicking on the left panel itself, or clicking on individual cells of the block.
Let me try clicking on the white part of the block (id=14) to see if MOUSE can change individual cell colors.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the white part of the block (id=14) to see if MOUSE changes individual cells
# id=14 is at top_left=[35,36], white, 35px
# Let me click on a cell within id=14
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 38}])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After clicking on white block part:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['id'] >= 10 and n['id'] <= 16:
        b = n['boundary']
        print(f"  id={n['id']}: color={n['color']} px={n['pixels']} top_left={b[0]}")
print(f"  board_changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")
print(f"  done: {last_action_result.get('done')}")
</parameter>
